questions.question:
When an array is passed as a parameter to a function which of the following statements is correct?



Programming in C: UGC NET CSE | June 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 48
options: The function can values in the original array
The function cannot values in the original array
questions.correct_answer: The function can values in the original array

questions.question:
What is the output of the following program: (Assume that the appropriate preprocessor directives are included and there is not syntax error)

main( )
    {   char S[]="ABCDEFGH";
        printf("%C", *(&S[3]));
        printf("%s", S+4);
        printf("%u", S);
    /*Base address of S is 1000 */
    }


Programming in C: UGC NET CSE | Junet 2015 | Part 2 | Question: 11

Suppose you are compiling on a machine with \( 1 \)-byte chars, \( 2 \)-byte shorts, \( 4 \)-byte ints, and \( 8 \)-byte doubles, and with alignment rules that require the address of every primitive data element to be an integer multiple of the element’s size. Suppose further that the compiler is not permitted to reorder fields; padding is used to ensure alignment. How much space will be consumed by the following array?

struct {
    short s;
    char c;
    short t;
    char d;
    double r;
    int i;
} A[10]; /*10 element array of structs */


Programming in C: UGC NET CSE | October 2020 | Part 2 | Question: 11
